compiled_state.title: Worktree cleanup: .tickets-tracker on tickets branch is eligible for removal -> ticket system data inaccessible after cleanup
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Problem

Worktree cleanup logic currently treats the `.tickets-tracker` worktree (on the `tickets` orphan branch) like any other worktree, making it a candidate for removal. This is incorrect — `.tickets-tracker` is specialized infrastructure for the ticket system (event-sourced tickets stored on the `tickets` orphan branch), not ephemeral development work.

## Expected Behavior

Worktree cleanup (both interactive `_offer_worktree_cleanup` in `claude-safe` and any other cleanup paths — e.g., `/dso:end`, retro, end-session) MUST exclude:

- The `.tickets-tracker/` worktree
- Any worktree checked out on the `tickets` branch

These should never be offered for cleanup and never be auto-removed, regardless of is_merged / is_clean status.

## Actual Behavior

Cleanup logic evaluates `.tickets-tracker` using the standard is_merged + is_clean heuristic. Because `tickets` is an orphan branch (and typically clean), it can pass the cleanup criteria and be removed.

## Scope

- Audit all worktree cleanup call sites for this exclusion.
- Add explicit skip logic keyed on branch name (`tickets`) and/or path (`.tickets-tracker`).
- Add a test verifying cleanup skips the tickets-tracker worktree even when it appears clean/merged.

## Impact

If cleaned up, the ticket system becomes inaccessible until re-checked out (`git worktree add .tickets-tracker tickets`). Silent data-access regression for any ticket CLI invocation.

comments.body: Triaged (debug-everything): orchestration/worktree bug. Fix spans multiple scripts; autonomous scope too broad. Deferred to end-of-session user review.
comments.author: Test
